_meta.verificationNote: Cyber risk assessment methodology constructed to align with NIST CSF 2.0 functions and NIST SP 800-30 Rev 1 guidance. Impact and likelihood scales are indicative examples for organizational adaptation.

title: Cyber Risk Assessment Methodology

alignedFrameworks.reference: Guide for Conducting Risk Assessments, September 2012

purpose: Establish a repeatable, structured approach to identifying, analyzing, evaluating, and treating cybersecurity risks. This methodology integrates with the NIST CSF 2.0 function hierarchy to ensure risk activities map directly to governance, identification, protection, detection, response, and recovery outcomes.
scope: All information systems, technology assets, data repositories, third-party services, and business processes within the organization's cybersecurity risk management boundary.

steps.name: Establish Context
steps.description: Define the organizational context, risk appetite, regulatory obligations, and stakeholder expectations that bound the risk assessment.
activities: Document organizational mission, objectives, and risk appetite
Identify applicable regulatory and contractual requirements
Define assessment scope and boundaries
Identify stakeholders and their risk tolerance thresholds
Establish risk evaluation criteria aligned to impact dimensions

steps.name: Identify Assets and Threats
steps.description: Inventory assets within scope, identify threat sources, and catalog threat events relevant to those assets.
steps.sp800_30_task: Task 2-1: Identify Threat Sources; Task 2-2: Identify Threat Events
activities: Maintain a comprehensive asset inventory (hardware, software, data, personnel, services)
Identify internal and external threat sources (adversarial, accidental, structural, environmental)
Catalog threat events relevant to identified assets and operations
Map assets to supporting business processes and mission functions
Identify dependencies and single points of failure

steps.name: Identify Vulnerabilities
steps.description: Assess existing vulnerabilities in assets, processes, and controls that could be exploited by identified threats.
steps.sp800_30_task: Task 2-3: Identify Vulnerabilities and Predisposing Conditions
activities: Conduct vulnerability assessments and penetration tests
Review configuration baselines and security control effectiveness
Analyze historical incident data for recurring vulnerability patterns
Assess predisposing conditions (architecture, technology, operational environment)
Map vulnerabilities to specific threat events

steps.name: Assess Existing Controls
steps.description: Evaluate the adequacy and effectiveness of current safeguards that protect against, detect, or respond to identified risks.
steps.sp800_30_task: Task 2-4: Determine Likelihood; Task 2-5: Determine Impact
activities: Inventory existing preventive controls (access controls, encryption, network segmentation)
Evaluate detective controls (monitoring, logging, alerting capabilities)
Assess response and recovery controls (incident response plans, backup procedures)
Determine control maturity using a defined maturity model
Identify control gaps and compensating controls

steps.name: Analyze Risk
steps.description: Determine likelihood and impact for each risk scenario considering threat capability, vulnerability severity, and existing control effectiveness.
steps.sp800_30_task: Task 2-4: Determine Likelihood; Task 2-5: Determine Impact; Task 2-6: Determine Risk
activities: Rate likelihood on a 5-point scale considering threat capability and control effectiveness
Rate impact on a 5-point scale across five dimensions
Calculate inherent risk (before additional controls)
Calculate residual risk (after existing controls)
Document risk analysis rationale and assumptions

steps.name: Evaluate and Prioritize
steps.description: Compare analyzed risks against the risk appetite and prioritize for treatment based on residual risk level and organizational impact.
activities: Rank risks by residual risk score
Compare residual risk levels against defined risk appetite
Identify risks requiring immediate treatment versus those within tolerance
Present risk evaluation results to governance stakeholders
Obtain risk acceptance or treatment decisions from risk owners

steps.name: Treat and Monitor
steps.description: Select and implement risk treatment strategies, then monitor for changes in risk posture and control effectiveness over time.
steps.sp800_30_task: Task 4-1: Monitor Risk Factors; Task 4-2: Update Risk Assessment
activities: Select treatment strategy (mitigate, transfer, accept, avoid) for each risk above appetite
Develop and assign treatment plans with owners, timelines, and success criteria
Implement additional controls or process changes
Establish ongoing monitoring of risk indicators and control effectiveness
Schedule periodic reassessment (quarterly or upon significant change)

likelihoodScale.description: Probability that a threat event will exploit a vulnerability, considering threat capability, intent, and existing control effectiveness. Aligned with NIST SP 800-30 Table H-3.

levels.label: Very Low
levels.description: The threat event is unlikely to be initiated or to succeed. Strong controls are in place and regularly validated.

levels.label: Low
levels.description: The threat event is possible but not expected. Adequate controls exist with minor gaps.

levels.label: Moderate
levels.description: The threat event is expected to occur at some point. Some controls exist but have known limitations.

levels.label: High
levels.description: The threat event is highly likely to occur. Controls are weak or have significant gaps.

levels.label: Very High
levels.description: The threat event is almost certain to occur or is already occurring. Controls are absent or ineffective.

impactScale.description: Magnitude of harm expected if a threat event occurs. Multi-dimensional assessment aligned with NIST SP 800-30 Table H-4. The highest impact across all dimensions determines the overall impact rating.

dimensions.description: Impact on the ability to deliver core services and maintain business operations.
dimensions.csfRelevance: Maps to RC (Recover) — extent of disruption determines recovery priority and effort.

dimensions.description: Direct and indirect monetary impact including remediation costs, lost revenue, penalties, and legal expenses.
dimensions.csfRelevance: Maps to GV.RM (Risk Management Strategy) — financial impact thresholds drive risk appetite decisions.

dimensions.description: Impact on regulatory compliance posture, including fines, sanctions, mandatory disclosures, and legal liability.
dimensions.csfRelevance: Maps to GV.OC (Organizational Context) — regulatory obligations shape control requirements.

dimensions.description: Impact on stakeholder trust, brand value, customer confidence, and market position.
dimensions.csfRelevance: Maps to RS.CO (Incident Response Communication) — communication quality directly affects reputational outcomes.

dimensions.description: Impact on human life, health, and physical safety of employees, customers, or the public.
dimensions.csfRelevance: Maps to PR.IR (Technology Infrastructure Resilience) — safety-critical systems require highest resilience standards.

levels.label: Negligible
levels.operational: Minor inconvenience, no measurable service disruption

levels.label: Minor
levels.operational: Brief disruption to non-critical services, workarounds available
levels.regulatory: Minor compliance gap, self-reported with no penalty

levels.label: Moderate
levels.operational: Significant disruption to one or more services, recovery within 24-72 hours
levels.regulatory: Regulatory notification required, potential for moderate fines

levels.label: Major
levels.operational: Extended outage of critical services, recovery requires days to weeks
levels.regulatory: Significant fines, regulatory investigation, potential sanctions
levels.reputation: Widespread media coverage, significant customer loss

levels.label: Catastrophic
levels.operational: Complete loss of critical services, potential organizational survival at risk
levels.regulatory: Severe penalties, license revocation, criminal liability
levels.reputation: Irreparable brand damage, mass customer exodus
levels.safety: Loss of life or permanent disability

reviewSchedule.fullAssessment: Annual, or upon significant organizational, technological, or threat landscape change
reviewSchedule.quarterlyReview: Review risk register, validate top-10 risks, update treatment progress
reviewSchedule.triggerBasedReview: Major incident, new threat intelligence, regulatory change, M&A activity, major system deployment
